```{python}
_stats_150k_s2 = make_scenario_stats(
    _o150k_s2_1, _d150k_s2_1, _t150k_s2_1, _r150k_s2_1,
    _o150k_s2_2, _d150k_s2_2, _t150k_s2_2, _r150k_s2_2,
    _o150k_s2_3, _d150k_s2_3, _t150k_s2_3, _r150k_s2_3,
    _norm_150k_s2, CONTROL_MEAN, _ptn_150k + MAX_TEST_N,
)
display(HTML(f"""<script>
window.__wif_scenarios = window.__wif_scenarios || {{}};
window.__wif_scenarios['150k_s2_cr10'] = {{
  row1:  {pio.to_json(_row_150k_s2)},
  stats: {json.dumps(_stats_150k_s2)}
}};
</script>"""))
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
# Scenario 150k: N=150k, σ=0.5 (reuse _df_s05)
_df_150k_s05 = se.calculate_launch_potential(_df_s05, post_test_n=_ptn_150k)
_df_150k_s05 = se.calculate_total_potential(_df_150k_s05)
_norm_150k_s05 = _df_150k_s05["total_trials_cumsum"].max() + _ptn_150k

_df_prepped_150k_s05 = prepare_stopping_decision_columns(
    _df_150k_s05.filter(pl.col("day") >= MIN_TEST_DAYS),
    metric_direction_improvement="increase",
).sort(["simulation", "day"])
_df_fixed_150k_s05 = prepare_stopping_decision_columns(
    _df_150k_s05.filter(pl.col("day") >= NUMBER_TEST_DAYS),
    metric_direction_improvement="increase",
).sort(["simulation", "day"])

_o150k_s05_1, _t150k_s05_1, _m150k_s05_1, _r150k_s05_1, _d150k_s05_1 = run_loop_df(_df_prepped_150k_s05, True)
_o150k_s05_2, _t150k_s05_2, _m150k_s05_2, _r150k_s05_2, _d150k_s05_2 = run_loop_df(_df_prepped_150k_s05, False)
_o150k_s05_3, _t150k_s05_3, _m150k_s05_3, _r150k_s05_3, _d150k_s05_3 = run_loop_df(_df_fixed_150k_s05, False)

_row_150k_s05   = make_scenario_row(
    _o150k_s05_1, _t150k_s05_1, _m150k_s05_1, _r150k_s05_1,
    _o150k_s05_2, _t150k_s05_2, _m150k_s05_2, _r150k_s05_2,
    _o150k_s05_3, _t150k_s05_3, _m150k_s05_3, _r150k_s05_3,
    _norm_150k_s05, CONTROL_MEAN, _ptn_150k + MAX_TEST_N,
)
_stats_150k_s05 = make_scenario_stats(
    _o150k_s05_1, _d150k_s05_1, _t150k_s05_1, _r150k_s05_1,
    _o150k_s05_2, _d150k_s05_2, _t150k_s05_2, _r150k_s05_2,
    _o150k_s05_3, _d150k_s05_3, _t150k_s05_3, _r150k_s05_3,
    _norm_150k_s05, CONTROL_MEAN, _ptn_150k + MAX_TEST_N,
)
display(HTML(f"""<script>
window.__wif_scenarios = window.__wif_scenarios || {{}};
window.__wif_scenarios['150k_s05_cr10'] = {{
  row1:  {pio.to_json(_row_150k_s05)},
  stats: {json.dumps(_stats_150k_s05)}
}};
</script>"""))
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
# Scenario 9: N=250k, σ=1 (reuse base df)
_ptn_250k_new = 200_000

_df_250k_new_s1 = se.calculate_launch_potential(df, post_test_n=_ptn_250k_new)
_df_250k_new_s1 = se.calculate_total_potential(_df_250k_new_s1)
_norm_250kn_s1 = _df_250k_new_s1["total_trials_cumsum"].max() + _ptn_250k_new

_df_prepped_250k_new_s1 = prepare_stopping_decision_columns(
    _df_250k_new_s1.filter(pl.col("day") >= MIN_TEST_DAYS),
    metric_direction_improvement="increase",
).sort(["simulation", "day"])
_df_fixed_250k_new_s1 = prepare_stopping_decision_columns(
    _df_250k_new_s1.filter(pl.col("day") >= NUMBER_TEST_DAYS),
    metric_direction_improvement="increase",
).sort(["simulation", "day"])

_o250kn_s1_1, _t250kn_s1_1, _m250kn_s1_1, _r250kn_s1_1, _d250kn_s1_1 = run_loop_df(_df_prepped_250k_new_s1, True)
_o250kn_s1_2, _t250kn_s1_2, _m250kn_s1_2, _r250kn_s1_2, _d250kn_s1_2 = run_loop_df(_df_prepped_250k_new_s1, False)
_o250kn_s1_3, _t250kn_s1_3, _m250kn_s1_3, _r250kn_s1_3, _d250kn_s1_3 = run_loop_df(_df_fixed_250k_new_s1, False)

_row_250kn_s1   = make_scenario_row(
    _o250kn_s1_1, _t250kn_s1_1, _m250kn_s1_1, _r250kn_s1_1,
    _o250kn_s1_2, _t250kn_s1_2, _m250kn_s1_2, _r250kn_s1_2,
    _o250kn_s1_3, _t250kn_s1_3, _m250kn_s1_3, _r250kn_s1_3,
    _norm_250kn_s1, CONTROL_MEAN, _ptn_250k_new + MAX_TEST_N,
)
_stats_250kn_s1 = make_scenario_stats(
    _o250kn_s1_1, _d250kn_s1_1, _t250kn_s1_1, _r250kn_s1_1,
    _o250kn_s1_2, _d250kn_s1_2, _t250kn_s1_2, _r250kn_s1_2,
    _o250kn_s1_3, _d250kn_s1_3, _t250kn_s1_3, _r250kn_s1_3,
    _norm_250kn_s1, CONTROL_MEAN, _ptn_250k_new + MAX_TEST_N,
)

display(HTML(f"""<script>
window.__wif_scenarios = window.__wif_scenarios || {{}};
window.__wif_scenarios['200k_s1_cr10'] = {{
  row1:  {pio.to_json(_row_250kn_s1)},
  stats: {json.dumps(_stats_250kn_s1)}
}};
</script>"""))
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
# Scenario 10: N=250k, σ=2 (reuse _df_wide)
_df_250k_new_s2 = se.calculate_launch_potential(_df_wide, post_test_n=_ptn_250k_new)
_df_250k_new_s2 = se.calculate_total_potential(_df_250k_new_s2)
_norm_250kn_s2 = _df_250k_new_s2["total_trials_cumsum"].max() + _ptn_250k_new

_df_prepped_250k_new_s2 = prepare_stopping_decision_columns(
    _df_250k_new_s2.filter(pl.col("day") >= MIN_TEST_DAYS),
    metric_direction_improvement="increase",
).sort(["simulation", "day"])
_df_fixed_250k_new_s2 = prepare_stopping_decision_columns(
    _df_250k_new_s2.filter(pl.col("day") >= NUMBER_TEST_DAYS),
    metric_direction_improvement="increase",
).sort(["simulation", "day"])

_o250kn_s2_1, _t250kn_s2_1, _m250kn_s2_1, _r250kn_s2_1, _d250kn_s2_1 = run_loop_df(_df_prepped_250k_new_s2, True)
_o250kn_s2_2, _t250kn_s2_2, _m250kn_s2_2, _r250kn_s2_2, _d250kn_s2_2 = run_loop_df(_df_prepped_250k_new_s2, False)
_o250kn_s2_3, _t250kn_s2_3, _m250kn_s2_3, _r250kn_s2_3, _d250kn_s2_3 = run_loop_df(_df_fixed_250k_new_s2, False)

_row_250kn_s2   = make_scenario_row(
    _o250kn_s2_1, _t250kn_s2_1, _m250kn_s2_1, _r250kn_s2_1,
    _o250kn_s2_2, _t250kn_s2_2, _m250kn_s2_2, _r250kn_s2_2,
    _o250kn_s2_3, _t250kn_s2_3, _m250kn_s2_3, _r250kn_s2_3,
    _norm_250kn_s2, CONTROL_MEAN, _ptn_250k_new + MAX_TEST_N,
)
_stats_250kn_s2 = make_scenario_stats(
    _o250kn_s2_1, _d250kn_s2_1, _t250kn_s2_1, _r250kn_s2_1,
    _o250kn_s2_2, _d250kn_s2_2, _t250kn_s2_2, _r250kn_s2_2,
    _o250kn_s2_3, _d250kn_s2_3, _t250kn_s2_3, _r250kn_s2_3,
    _norm_250kn_s2, CONTROL_MEAN, _ptn_250k_new + MAX_TEST_N,
)

display(HTML(f"""<script>
window.__wif_scenarios = window.__wif_scenarios || {{}};
window.__wif_scenarios['200k_s2_cr10'] = {{
  row1:  {pio.to_json(_row_250kn_s2)},
  stats: {json.dumps(_stats_250kn_s2)}
}};
</script>"""))
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
# Scenario 11: N=250k, σ=0.5 (reuse _df_s05)
_df_250k_new_s05 = se.calculate_launch_potential(_df_s05, post_test_n=_ptn_250k_new)
_df_250k_new_s05 = se.calculate_total_potential(_df_250k_new_s05)
_norm_250kn_s05 = _df_250k_new_s05["total_trials_cumsum"].max() + _ptn_250k_new

_df_prepped_250k_new_s05 = prepare_stopping_decision_columns(
    _df_250k_new_s05.filter(pl.col("day") >= MIN_TEST_DAYS),
    metric_direction_improvement="increase",
).sort(["simulation", "day"])
_df_fixed_250k_new_s05 = prepare_stopping_decision_columns(
    _df_250k_new_s05.filter(pl.col("day") >= NUMBER_TEST_DAYS),
    metric_direction_improvement="increase",
).sort(["simulation", "day"])

_o250kn_s05_1, _t250kn_s05_1, _m250kn_s05_1, _r250kn_s05_1, _d250kn_s05_1 = run_loop_df(_df_prepped_250k_new_s05, True)
_o250kn_s05_2, _t250kn_s05_2, _m250kn_s05_2, _r250kn_s05_2, _d250kn_s05_2 = run_loop_df(_df_prepped_250k_new_s05, False)
_o250kn_s05_3, _t250kn_s05_3, _m250kn_s05_3, _r250kn_s05_3, _d250kn_s05_3 = run_loop_df(_df_fixed_250k_new_s05, False)

_row_250kn_s05   = make_scenario_row(
    _o250kn_s05_1, _t250kn_s05_1, _m250kn_s05_1, _r250kn_s05_1,
    _o250kn_s05_2, _t250kn_s05_2, _m250kn_s05_2, _r250kn_s05_2,
    _o250kn_s05_3, _t250kn_s05_3, _m250kn_s05_3, _r250kn_s05_3,
    _norm_250kn_s05, CONTROL_MEAN, _ptn_250k_new + MAX_TEST_N,
)
_stats_250kn_s05 = make_scenario_stats(
    _o250kn_s05_1, _d250kn_s05_1, _t250kn_s05_1, _r250kn_s05_1,
    _o250kn_s05_2, _d250kn_s05_2, _t250kn_s05_2, _r250kn_s05_2,
    _o250kn_s05_3, _d250kn_s05_3, _t250kn_s05_3, _r250kn_s05_3,
    _norm_250kn_s05, CONTROL_MEAN, _ptn_250k_new + MAX_TEST_N,
)

display(HTML(f"""<script>
window.__wif_scenarios = window.__wif_scenarios || {{}};
window.__wif_scenarios['200k_s05_cr10'] = {{
  row1:  {pio.to_json(_row_250kn_s05)},
  stats: {json.dumps(_stats_250kn_s05)}
}};
</script>"""))
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
# Scenario 12: N=1600k, σ=1 (reuse base df)
_ptn_1600k = 1_600_000

_df_1600k_s1 = se.calculate_launch_potential(df, post_test_n=_ptn_1600k)
_df_1600k_s1 = se.calculate_total_potential(_df_1600k_s1)
_norm_1600k_s1 = _df_1600k_s1["total_trials_cumsum"].max() + _ptn_1600k

_df_prepped_1600k_s1 = prepare_stopping_decision_columns(
    _df_1600k_s1.filter(pl.col("day") >= MIN_TEST_DAYS),
    metric_direction_improvement="increase",
).sort(["simulation", "day"])
_df_fixed_1600k_s1 = prepare_stopping_decision_columns(
    _df_1600k_s1.filter(pl.col("day") >= NUMBER_TEST_DAYS),
    metric_direction_improvement="increase",
).sort(["simulation", "day"])

_o1600k_s1_1, _t1600k_s1_1, _m1600k_s1_1, _r1600k_s1_1, _d1600k_s1_1 = run_loop_df(_df_prepped_1600k_s1, True)
_o1600k_s1_2, _t1600k_s1_2, _m1600k_s1_2, _r1600k_s1_2, _d1600k_s1_2 = run_loop_df(_df_prepped_1600k_s1, False)
_o1600k_s1_3, _t1600k_s1_3, _m1600k_s1_3, _r1600k_s1_3, _d1600k_s1_3 = run_loop_df(_df_fixed_1600k_s1, False)

_row_1600k_s1   = make_scenario_row(
    _o1600k_s1_1, _t1600k_s1_1, _m1600k_s1_1, _r1600k_s1_1,
    _o1600k_s1_2, _t1600k_s1_2, _m1600k_s1_2, _r1600k_s1_2,
    _o1600k_s1_3, _t1600k_s1_3, _m1600k_s1_3, _r1600k_s1_3,
    _norm_1600k_s1, CONTROL_MEAN, _ptn_1600k + MAX_TEST_N,
)
_stats_1600k_s1 = make_scenario_stats(
    _o1600k_s1_1, _d1600k_s1_1, _t1600k_s1_1, _r1600k_s1_1,
    _o1600k_s1_2, _d1600k_s1_2, _t1600k_s1_2, _r1600k_s1_2,
    _o1600k_s1_3, _d1600k_s1_3, _t1600k_s1_3, _r1600k_s1_3,
    _norm_1600k_s1, CONTROL_MEAN, _ptn_1600k + MAX_TEST_N,
)
display(HTML(f"""<script>
window.__wif_scenarios = window.__wif_scenarios || {{}};
window.__wif_scenarios['1600k_s1_cr10'] = {{
  row1:  {pio.to_json(_row_1600k_s1)},
  stats: {json.dumps(_stats_1600k_s1)}
}};
</script>"""))
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
# Scenario 13: N=1600k, σ=2 (reuse _df_wide)
_df_1600k_s2 = se.calculate_launch_potential(_df_wide, post_test_n=_ptn_1600k)
_df_1600k_s2 = se.calculate_total_potential(_df_1600k_s2)
_norm_1600k_s2 = _df_1600k_s2["total_trials_cumsum"].max() + _ptn_1600k

_df_prepped_1600k_s2 = prepare_stopping_decision_columns(
    _df_1600k_s2.filter(pl.col("day") >= MIN_TEST_DAYS),
    metric_direction_improvement="increase",
).sort(["simulation", "day"])
_df_fixed_1600k_s2 = prepare_stopping_decision_columns(
    _df_1600k_s2.filter(pl.col("day") >= NUMBER_TEST_DAYS),
    metric_direction_improvement="increase",
).sort(["simulation", "day"])

_o1600k_s2_1, _t1600k_s2_1, _m1600k_s2_1, _r1600k_s2_1, _d1600k_s2_1 = run_loop_df(_df_prepped_1600k_s2, True)
_o1600k_s2_2, _t1600k_s2_2, _m1600k_s2_2, _r1600k_s2_2, _d1600k_s2_2 = run_loop_df(_df_prepped_1600k_s2, False)
_o1600k_s2_3, _t1600k_s2_3, _m1600k_s2_3, _r1600k_s2_3, _d1600k_s2_3 = run_loop_df(_df_fixed_1600k_s2, False)

_row_1600k_s2   = make_scenario_row(
    _o1600k_s2_1, _t1600k_s2_1, _m1600k_s2_1, _r1600k_s2_1,
    _o1600k_s2_2, _t1600k_s2_2, _m1600k_s2_2, _r1600k_s2_2,
    _o1600k_s2_3, _t1600k_s2_3, _m1600k_s2_3, _r1600k_s2_3,
    _norm_1600k_s2, CONTROL_MEAN, _ptn_1600k + MAX_TEST_N,
)
_stats_1600k_s2 = make_scenario_stats(
    _o1600k_s2_1, _d1600k_s2_1, _t1600k_s2_1, _r1600k_s2_1,
    _o1600k_s2_2, _d1600k_s2_2, _t1600k_s2_2, _r1600k_s2_2,
    _o1600k_s2_3, _d1600k_s2_3, _t1600k_s2_3, _r1600k_s2_3,
    _norm_1600k_s2, CONTROL_MEAN, _ptn_1600k + MAX_TEST_N,
)
display(HTML(f"""<script>
window.__wif_scenarios = window.__wif_scenarios || {{}};
window.__wif_scenarios['1600k_s2_cr10'] = {{
  row1:  {pio.to_json(_row_1600k_s2)},
  stats: {json.dumps(_stats_1600k_s2)}
}};
</script>"""))
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
# Scenario 14: N=1600k, σ=0.5 (reuse _df_s05)
_df_1600k_s05 = se.calculate_launch_potential(_df_s05, post_test_n=_ptn_1600k)
_df_1600k_s05 = se.calculate_total_potential(_df_1600k_s05)
_norm_1600k_s05 = _df_1600k_s05["total_trials_cumsum"].max() + _ptn_1600k

_df_prepped_1600k_s05 = prepare_stopping_decision_columns(
    _df_1600k_s05.filter(pl.col("day") >= MIN_TEST_DAYS),
    metric_direction_improvement="increase",
).sort(["simulation", "day"])
_df_fixed_1600k_s05 = prepare_stopping_decision_columns(
    _df_1600k_s05.filter(pl.col("day") >= NUMBER_TEST_DAYS),
    metric_direction_improvement="increase",
).sort(["simulation", "day"])

_o1600k_s05_1, _t1600k_s05_1, _m1600k_s05_1, _r1600k_s05_1, _d1600k_s05_1 = run_loop_df(_df_prepped_1600k_s05, True)
_o1600k_s05_2, _t1600k_s05_2, _m1600k_s05_2, _r1600k_s05_2, _d1600k_s05_2 = run_loop_df(_df_prepped_1600k_s05, False)
_o1600k_s05_3, _t1600k_s05_3, _m1600k_s05_3, _r1600k_s05_3, _d1600k_s05_3 = run_loop_df(_df_fixed_1600k_s05, False)

_row_1600k_s05   = make_scenario_row(
    _o1600k_s05_1, _t1600k_s05_1, _m1600k_s05_1, _r1600k_s05_1,
    _o1600k_s05_2, _t1600k_s05_2, _m1600k_s05_2, _r1600k_s05_2,
    _o1600k_s05_3, _t1600k_s05_3, _m1600k_s05_3, _r1600k_s05_3,
    _norm_1600k_s05, CONTROL_MEAN, _ptn_1600k + MAX_TEST_N,
)
_stats_1600k_s05 = make_scenario_stats(
    _o1600k_s05_1, _d1600k_s05_1, _t1600k_s05_1, _r1600k_s05_1,
    _o1600k_s05_2, _d1600k_s05_2, _t1600k_s05_2, _r1600k_s05_2,
    _o1600k_s05_3, _d1600k_s05_3, _t1600k_s05_3, _r1600k_s05_3,
    _norm_1600k_s05, CONTROL_MEAN, _ptn_1600k + MAX_TEST_N,
)
display(HTML(f"""<script>
window.__wif_scenarios = window.__wif_scenarios || {{}};
window.__wif_scenarios['1600k_s05_cr10'] = {{
  row1:  {pio.to_json(_row_1600k_s05)},
  stats: {json.dumps(_stats_1600k_s05)}
}};
</script>"""))
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
# Additional baseline conversion rates: 5%, 30%, and 65%

def _peak_stats_cr(outcomes, avg_days_list, norm, cr_val, ptn_val, round_peak=False):
    y    = np.array(outcomes) / norm
    x    = p_thresholds
    peak = int(np.argmax(np.round(y * 100, 4)) if round_peak else np.argmax(y))
    opt_pr_best     = x[peak]
    marginal_events = (y[peak] - cr_val) * (ptn_val + MAX_TEST_N)
    avg_d           = avg_days_list[peak]
    return f"""<div style="text-align:center; font-size:0.9rem; color:#333; margin-top:0.25rem;">
      Pr(Best): <strong>{opt_pr_best:.2f}</strong> &nbsp;|&nbsp;
      Marginal Events vs No Test: <strong>{marginal_events:,.0f}</strong> &nbsp;|&nbsp;
      Avg(Days): <strong>{avg_d:.1f}</strong>
    </div>"""

_cr_combos    = [(0.05, 'cr05'), (0.25, 'cr25'), (0.40, 'cr40'), (0.60, 'cr60'), (0.80, 'cr80')]
_sigma_combos = [(ITC_SE, 's1'), (2, 's2'), (0.5, 's05')]
_ptn_combos   = [(50_000, '50k'), (100_000, '100k'), (150_000, '150k'), (200_000, '200k'), (POST_TEST_N, '400k'), (POST_TEST_N * 2, '800k'), (1_600_000, '1600k')]

for _cr_val, _cr_key in _cr_combos:
    for _itc_se_val, _sigma_key in _sigma_combos:
        _n_sims_loop = NUMBER_SIMULATIONS
        _df_tmp = se.create_simulation_df(
            number_test_days=NUMBER_TEST_DAYS,
            number_simulations=_n_sims_loop,
            seed=SEED,
            condition_parameters=[{"mean": _cr_val, "trials": int(MAX_TEST_N * SPLIT)}],
        )
        _params_tmp = {"itc_mean": ITC_MEAN, "itc_se": _itc_se_val, "trials": int(MAX_TEST_N * SPLIT)}
        _df_tmp = se.add_random_effect_condition(_df_tmp, _params_tmp)
        _df_tmp = se.add_cumulative_columns(_df_tmp)
        _df_tmp = se.compute_cumulative_std_rates(_df_tmp)
        _df_tmp = se.compute_delta_stats(_df_tmp)
        _df_tmp = se.compute_test_stats(_df_tmp)
        _df_tmp = se.calculate_remaining_test_potential(_df_tmp)

        for _ptn_val, _ptn_key in _ptn_combos:
            _df_ptn = se.calculate_launch_potential(_df_tmp, post_test_n=_ptn_val)
            _df_ptn = se.calculate_total_potential(_df_ptn)
            _norm   = _df_ptn["total_trials_cumsum"].max() + _ptn_val

            _df_prepped_cr = prepare_stopping_decision_columns(
                _df_ptn.filter(pl.col("day") >= MIN_TEST_DAYS),
                metric_direction_improvement="increase",
            ).sort(["simulation", "day"])

            _df_fixed_cr = prepare_stopping_decision_columns(
                _df_ptn.filter(pl.col("day") >= NUMBER_TEST_DAYS),
                metric_direction_improvement="increase",
            ).sort(["simulation", "day"])

            _o1, _t1, _m1, _r1, _d1 = run_loop_df(_df_prepped_cr, True)
            _o2, _t2, _m2, _r2, _d2 = run_loop_df(_df_prepped_cr, False)
            _o3, _t3, _m3, _r3, _d3 = run_loop_df(_df_fixed_cr, False)

            _row_cr   = make_scenario_row(
                _o1, _t1, _m1, _r1,
                _o2, _t2, _m2, _r2,
                _o3, _t3, _m3, _r3,
                _norm, _cr_val, _ptn_val + MAX_TEST_N,
            )
            _stats_cr = make_scenario_stats(
                _o1, _d1, _t1, _r1, _o2, _d2, _t2, _r2, _o3, _d3, _t3, _r3,
                _norm, _cr_val, _ptn_val + MAX_TEST_N,
            )

            _key = f"{_ptn_key}_{_sigma_key}_{_cr_key}"
            display(HTML(f"""<script>
window.__wif_scenarios = window.__wif_scenarios || {{}};
window.__wif_scenarios['{_key}'] = {{
  row1:  {pio.to_json(_row_cr)},
  stats: {json.dumps(_stats_cr)}
}};
</script>"""))
```

## What the Simulations Say

### 1. Fixed Sample Testing at α = 0.50 is a Good Strategy

```{=html}
<p style="font-size:0.95rem; color:#444; margin-bottom:0.75rem;">
  Conventional α = 0.05 minimizes false positives. α = 0.50 maximizes value — deploy whoever is leading on the final day.
</p>

<table id="wts-ratio-stat" style="font-size:0.88rem; border-collapse:collapse; margin-bottom:0.6rem;">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th style="padding:5px 14px; color:#666; font-weight:600; border-bottom:1px solid #ddd; text-align:left;">Strategy</th>
      <th style="padding:5px 14px; color:#666; font-weight:600; border-bottom:1px solid #ddd; text-align:center;">Projected Mean</th>
      <th style="padding:5px 14px; color:#666; font-weight:600; border-bottom:1px solid #ddd; text-align:center;">Marginal Events</th>
      <th style="padding:5px 14px; color:#666; font-weight:600; border-bottom:1px solid #ddd; text-align:center;">Avg Days</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody id="wts-ratio-tbody">
    <tr><td colspan="4" style="padding:5px 14px; color:#aaa;">loading…</td></tr>
  </tbody>
</table>
<p id="wts-ratio-note" style="font-size:0.82rem; color:#666; font-style:italic; margin-top:0.25rem;"></p>

<script>
(function () {
  function getStat(key, label, isRef) {
    var sc = window.__wif_scenarios && window.__wif_scenarios[key];
    if (!sc) return null;
    return sc.stats.find(function (r) { return r.label === label && r.is_ref === isRef; }) || null;
  }

  function row(d, label, highlight) {
    var bg = highlight ? 'background:#f0f6ff;' : '';
    return '<tr style="' + bg + '">' +
      '<td style="padding:5px 14px; font-weight:600; color:' + d.color + ';">' + label + '</td>' +
      '<td style="padding:5px 14px; text-align:center; font-weight:700;">' + (d.proj_mean * 100).toFixed(3) + '%</td>' +
      '<td style="padding:5px 14px; text-align:center; font-weight:700;">' + Math.round(d.marginal).toLocaleString() + '</td>' +
      '<td style="padding:5px 14px; text-align:center; font-weight:700;">' + d.avg_days.toFixed(1) + '</td>' +
      '</tr>';
  }

  function update() {
    var cr    = (document.getElementById('wif-cr')    || {}).value || 'cr10';
    var sigma = (document.getElementById('wif-sigma') || {}).value || 's1';
    var ptn   = (document.getElementById('wif-ptn')   || {}).value || '400k';
    var key   = ptn + '_' + sigma + '_' + cr;
    var opt   = getStat(key, 'Fixed Sample', false);
    var ref   = getStat(key, 'Fixed Sample', true);
    if (!opt || !ref || !ref.marginal) return;
    var ratio = opt.marginal / ref.marginal;
    var color = ratio >= 2 ? '#1a7a3c' : ratio >= 1.3 ? '#2d6fa3' : '#555';
    document.getElementById('wts-ratio-tbody').innerHTML =
      row(ref, 'Fixed Sample (α = 0.05)', false) +
      row(opt, 'Fixed Sample (α = 0.50)', true);
    var note = document.getElementById('wts-ratio-note');
    if (note) {
      note.textContent = '* α = 0.50 captures ';
      note.innerHTML   = '* α = 0.50 captures <strong style="color:' + color + ';">' +
        ratio.toFixed(2) + '×</strong> the marginal events of α = 0.05, in the same number of days.';
    }
  }

  function waitAndRender() {
    if (!window.__wif_scenarios) { setTimeout(waitAndRender, 300); return; }
    update();
  }

  ['wif-cr', 'wif-sigma', 'wif-ptn'].forEach(function (id) {
    var el = document.getElementById(id);
    if (el) el.addEventListener('change', update);
  });

  if (document.readyState === 'loading') {
    document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', waitAndRender);
  } else {
    setTimeout(waitAndRender, 0);
  }
})();
</script>
```

### 2. Peeking is a Good Strategy

```{=html}
<p style="font-size:0.95rem; color:#444; margin-bottom:0.75rem;">
  Peeking and Fixed Sample at α = 0.50 perform comparably — but peeking experiments end sooner.
</p>

<table id="wts-peek-stat" style="font-size:0.88rem; border-collapse:collapse; margin-bottom:0.6rem;">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th style="padding:5px 14px; color:#666; font-weight:600; border-bottom:1px solid #ddd; text-align:left;">Strategy</th>
      <th style="padding:5px 14px; color:#666; font-weight:600; border-bottom:1px solid #ddd; text-align:center;">Projected Mean</th>
      <th style="padding:5px 14px; color:#666; font-weight:600; border-bottom:1px solid #ddd; text-align:center;">Marginal Events</th>
      <th style="padding:5px 14px; color:#666; font-weight:600; border-bottom:1px solid #ddd; text-align:center;">Avg Days</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody id="wts-peek-tbody">
    <tr><td colspan="4" style="padding:5px 14px; color:#aaa;">loading…</td></tr>
  </tbody>
</table>
<p id="wts-peek-note" style="font-size:0.82rem; color:#666; font-style:italic; margin-top:0.25rem;"></p>
<p style="font-size:0.82rem; color:#777; margin-top:0.4rem;">Peeking makes more individual mistakes — stopping early on noisy signals picks the wrong winner more often than waiting for the full sample. But those mistakes are priced into the long-run value score. What peeking gains by ending experiments early offsets what it loses in accuracy.</p>

<script>
(function () {
  function getStat(key, label, isRef) {
    var sc = window.__wif_scenarios && window.__wif_scenarios[key];
    if (!sc) return null;
    return sc.stats.find(function (r) { return r.label === label && r.is_ref === isRef; }) || null;
  }

  function row(d, highlight) {
    var bg = highlight ? 'background:#f0f6ff;' : '';
    return '<tr style="' + bg + '">' +
      '<td style="padding:5px 14px; font-weight:600; color:' + d.color + ';">' + d.label + '</td>' +
      '<td style="padding:5px 14px; text-align:center; font-weight:700;">' + (d.proj_mean * 100).toFixed(3) + '%</td>' +
      '<td style="padding:5px 14px; text-align:center; font-weight:700;">' + Math.round(d.marginal).toLocaleString() + '</td>' +
      '<td style="padding:5px 14px; text-align:center; font-weight:700;">' + d.avg_days.toFixed(1) + '</td>' +
      '</tr>';
  }

  function update() {
    var cr    = (document.getElementById('wif-cr')    || {}).value || 'cr10';
    var sigma = (document.getElementById('wif-sigma') || {}).value || 's1';
    var ptn   = (document.getElementById('wif-ptn')   || {}).value || '400k';
    var key   = ptn + '_' + sigma + '_' + cr;
    var peek  = getStat(key, 'Peeking',       false);
    var fs    = getStat(key, 'Fixed Sample',  false);
    if (!peek || !fs) return;
    document.getElementById('wts-peek-tbody').innerHTML = row(fs, false) + row(peek, true);
    var daysSaved = fs.avg_days - peek.avg_days;
    var note = document.getElementById('wts-peek-note');
    if (note) note.textContent = '* Peeking finishes ~' + Math.abs(daysSaved).toFixed(1) +
      ' days faster on average while delivering ' +
      (Math.abs((peek.marginal - fs.marginal) / fs.marginal * 100)).toFixed(1) +
      '% ' + (peek.marginal >= fs.marginal ? 'more' : 'fewer') + ' marginal events.';
  }

  function waitAndRender() {
    if (!window.__wif_scenarios) { setTimeout(waitAndRender, 300); return; }
    update();
  }

  ['wif-cr', 'wif-sigma', 'wif-ptn'].forEach(function (id) {
    var el = document.getElementById(id);
    if (el) el.addEventListener('change', update);
  });

  if (document.readyState === 'loading') {
    document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', waitAndRender);
  } else {
    setTimeout(waitAndRender, 0);
  }
})();
</script>
```

### 3. Peeking + Retaining the Leader Is Best

```{=html}
<p style="font-size:0.95rem; color:#444; margin-bottom:0.75rem;">
  Peeking stops experiments early when the data says so. Retaining the best performer means every inconclusive test still ships a winner. Together, they dominate.
</p>

<table id="wts-best-stat" style="font-size:0.88rem; border-collapse:collapse; margin-bottom:0.6rem;">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th style="padding:5px 14px; color:#666; font-weight:600; border-bottom:1px solid #ddd; text-align:left;">Strategy</th>
      <th style="padding:5px 14px; color:#666; font-weight:600; border-bottom:1px solid #ddd; text-align:center;">Projected Mean</th>
      <th style="padding:5px 14px; color:#666; font-weight:600; border-bottom:1px solid #ddd; text-align:center;">Marginal Events</th>
      <th style="padding:5px 14px; color:#666; font-weight:600; border-bottom:1px solid #ddd; text-align:center;">Correct Decision Rate</th>
      <th style="padding:5px 14px; color:#666; font-weight:600; border-bottom:1px solid #ddd; text-align:center;">Avg Days</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody id="wts-best-tbody">
    <tr><td colspan="5" style="padding:5px 14px; color:#aaa;">loading…</td></tr>
  </tbody>
</table>
<p id="wts-best-note" style="font-size:0.82rem; color:#666; font-style:italic; margin-top:0.25rem;"></p>

<script>
(function () {
  function getStat(key, label, isRef) {
    var sc = window.__wif_scenarios && window.__wif_scenarios[key];
    if (!sc) return null;
    return sc.stats.find(function (r) { return r.label === label && r.is_ref === isRef; }) || null;
  }

  function row(d, highlight) {
    var bg = highlight ? 'background:#f0f6ff;' : '';
    return '<tr style="' + bg + '">' +
      '<td style="padding:5px 14px; font-weight:600; color:' + d.color + ';">' + d.label + '</td>' +
      '<td style="padding:5px 14px; text-align:center; font-weight:700;">' + (d.proj_mean * 100).toFixed(3) + '%</td>' +
      '<td style="padding:5px 14px; text-align:center; font-weight:700;">' + Math.round(d.marginal).toLocaleString() + '</td>' +
      '<td style="padding:5px 14px; text-align:center; font-weight:700;">' + (d.best_rate * 100).toFixed(1) + '%</td>' +
      '<td style="padding:5px 14px; text-align:center; font-weight:700;">' + d.avg_days.toFixed(1) + '</td>' +
      '</tr>';
  }

  function update() {
    var cr    = (document.getElementById('wif-cr')    || {}).value || 'cr10';
    var sigma = (document.getElementById('wif-sigma') || {}).value || 's1';
    var ptn   = (document.getElementById('wif-ptn')   || {}).value || '400k';
    var key   = ptn + '_' + sigma + '_' + cr;
    var pb    = getStat(key, 'Peeking + Best', false);
    var peek  = getStat(key, 'Peeking',        false);
    var fs    = getStat(key, 'Fixed Sample',   false);
    if (!pb || !peek || !fs) return;
    document.getElementById('wts-best-tbody').innerHTML = row(fs, false) + row(peek, false) + row(pb, true);
    var liftVsPeek = (pb.marginal - peek.marginal) / Math.abs(peek.marginal) * 100;
    var liftVsFs   = (pb.marginal - fs.marginal)   / Math.abs(fs.marginal)   * 100;
    var daysDiff   = pb.avg_days - peek.avg_days;
    var note = document.getElementById('wts-best-note');
    if (note) note.innerHTML =
      '* Peeking+Best captures <strong>' + Math.abs(liftVsPeek).toFixed(1) + '%</strong> ' +
      (liftVsPeek >= 0 ? 'more' : 'fewer') + ' marginal events than Peeking and ' +
      '<strong>' + Math.abs(liftVsFs).toFixed(1) + '%</strong> ' +
      (liftVsFs >= 0 ? 'more' : 'fewer') + ' than Fixed Sample (α = 0.50), ' +
      'running ' + Math.abs(daysDiff).toFixed(1) + ' days ' +
      (daysDiff >= 0 ? 'longer' : 'shorter') + ' than Peeking on average.';
  }

  function waitAndRender() {
    if (!window.__wif_scenarios) { setTimeout(waitAndRender, 300); return; }
    update();
  }

  ['wif-cr', 'wif-sigma', 'wif-ptn'].forEach(function (id) {
    var el = document.getElementById(id);
    if (el) el.addEventListener('change', update);
  });

  if (document.readyState === 'loading') {
    document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', waitAndRender);
  } else {
    setTimeout(waitAndRender, 0);
  }
})();
</script>
```

## Importance of Post-Test N

The larger the post-test audience, the more costly a wrong call. The stopping threshold rises to match — making early stops harder to earn as the stakes grow.

```{=html}
<div style="margin-bottom:1.5rem;">

  <div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.6rem; margin-bottom:0.75rem; border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-radius:6px; padding:0.5rem 1rem;">
    <div style="font-size:1.05rem; color:#444; font-style:italic;">Change the scenario — notice whether the peaks shift or hold.</div>
    <div style="display:flex; gap:2rem; align-items:flex-start; flex-wrap:wrap;">
    <div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.25rem;">
      <label style="font-size:0.85rem; color:#555; font-weight:600;">Baseline Conversion</label>
      <select id="ptn-cr" class="form-select form-select-sm" style="width:auto; font-size:0.85rem;">
        <option value="cr05">5%</option>
        <option value="cr10" selected>10%</option>
        <option value="cr25">25%</option>
        <option value="cr40">40%</option>
        <option value="cr60">60%</option>
        <option value="cr80">80%</option>
      </select>
    </div>
    <div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.25rem;">
      <label style="font-size:0.85rem; color:#555; font-weight:600;">Expected Effects</label>
      <select id="ptn-sigma" class="form-select form-select-sm" style="width:auto; font-size:0.85rem;">
        <option value="s05">ITC ~ N(100, 0.5)</option>
        <option value="s1" selected>ITC ~ N(100, 1)</option>
        <option value="s2">ITC ~ N(100, 2)</option>
      </select>
    </div>
    <div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.25rem;">
      <label style="font-size:0.85rem; color:#555; font-weight:600;">Metric</label>
      <select id="ptn-metric" class="form-select form-select-sm" style="width:auto; font-size:0.85rem;">
        <option value="alpha" selected>Optimal Pr(Best)</option>
        <option value="days">Avg Days</option>
        <option value="marginal">Marginal Events</option>
      </select>
    </div>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div id="ptn-alpha-chart"></div>

  <div id="ptn-takeaways-box" style="margin-top:0.75rem; padding:0.65rem 1rem; background:#f8f9fa; border-radius:6px; border-left:3px solid #3a6fd8; font-size:0.9rem; color:#333; line-height:1.75;">
    <ul id="ptn-takeaways" style="margin:0; padding-left:1.2rem;"></ul>
  </div>

</div>

<script>
(function () {
  var PTN_VALS      = [50000, 100000, 150000, 200000, 400000, 800000, 1600000];
  var PTN_KEYS      = ['50k', '100k', '150k', '200k', '400k', '800k', '1600k'];
  var PTN_LABELS    = ['50k', '100k', '150k', '200k', '400k', '800k', '1600k'];
  var PTN_TICK_LBLS = ['50k', '', '', '200k', '400k', '800k', '1600k'];
  var PAL        = ['#1f77b4', '#ff7f0e', '#2ca02c'];
  var STRATS     = ['Peeking + Best', 'Peeking', 'Fixed Sample'];

  function getStat(ptn_key, sigma_key, cr_key, label, isRef) {
    var key = ptn_key + '_' + sigma_key + '_' + cr_key;
    var sc  = window.__wif_scenarios && window.__wif_scenarios[key];
    if (!sc) return null;
    return sc.stats.find(function (r) { return r.label === label && r.is_ref === isRef; }) || null;
  }

  function buildChart(cr_key, sigma_key, metric) {
    var traces = [];
    var isAlpha = (metric === 'alpha');
    var isDays  = (metric === 'days');

    STRATS.forEach(function (lbl, i) {
      var vals = [], ht = [];
      PTN_KEYS.forEach(function (pk, j) {
        var s = getStat(pk, sigma_key, cr_key, lbl, false);
        if (s) {
          vals.push(isDays ? s.avg_days : (isAlpha ? s.alpha : s.marginal));
          ht.push(lbl + '<br>Post-Test N: ' + PTN_LABELS[j] +
            '<br>Optimal Pr(Best): ' + s.alpha.toFixed(2) +
            '<br>Avg Days: ' + s.avg_days.toFixed(1) +
            '<br>Marginal Events: ' + Math.round(s.marginal).toLocaleString() +
            '<extra></extra>');
        }
      });
      traces.push({
        x: isBar ? PTN_LABELS : PTN_VALS, y: vals, mode: 'lines+markers', name: lbl,
        visible: (!isAlpha && !isDays) || (lbl === 'Peeking + Best') ? true : 'legendonly',
        line: { color: PAL[i], width: 2 },
        marker: { symbol: 'square', size: 9, color: PAL[i] },
        hovertemplate: ht
      });
    });

    var annotations = [], extraRight = 55;

    if (isAlpha) {
    } else if (isDays) {
      traces.push({
        x: PTN_VALS, y: [20, 20, 20, 20, 20, 20, 20], mode: 'lines',
        name: 'Max test days (20)', showlegend: false,
        line: { color: '#aaa', width: 1.5, dash: 'dot' },
        hovertemplate: 'Max test days = 20<extra></extra>'
      });
      annotations.push({
        x: PTN_VALS[PTN_VALS.length - 1], y: 20, xref: 'x', yref: 'y',
        text: 'Day 20', showarrow: false,
        font: { color: '#999', size: 11 },
        xanchor: 'left', yanchor: 'bottom', xshift: 6
      });
    } else {
      // marginal: Fixed@0.05 reference bar
      var ref_m = [], ref_ht = [];
      PTN_KEYS.forEach(function (pk, j) {
        var s = getStat(pk, sigma_key, cr_key, 'Fixed Sample', true);
        ref_m.push(s ? s.marginal : null);
        ref_ht.push(s ? 'Fixed @ Pr(Best)=0.95<br>Post-Test N: ' + PTN_LABELS[j] +
          '<br>Marginal Events: ' + Math.round(s.marginal).toLocaleString() +
          '<extra></extra>' : '<extra></extra>');
      });
      traces.push({
        x: PTN_LABELS, y: ref_m, type: 'bar',
        name: 'Fixed @ Pr(Best)=0.95',
        marker: { color: 'rgba(44,160,44,0.35)', line: { color: '#2ca02c', width: 1.5 } },
        hovertemplate: ref_ht
      });
    }

    var isBar = (metric === 'marginal');
    // re-cast strategy traces as bars for marginal view
    if (isBar) {
      traces = traces.map(function (t) {
        if (t.type === 'bar') return t;
        var barName = t.name === 'Fixed Sample' ? 'Fixed Sample @ Pr(Best) = 0.50' : t.name;
        return Object.assign({}, t, {
          type: 'bar',
          x: PTN_LABELS,
          name: barName,
          marker: { color: t.line.color }
        });
      });
    }

    Plotly.react('ptn-alpha-chart', traces, {
      barmode: isBar ? 'group' : undefined,
      xaxis: isBar ? {
        title: { text: 'Post-Test N', font: { size: 12 }, standoff: 4 },
        ticktext: PTN_TICK_LBLS, tickvals: PTN_LABELS,
        showgrid: false
      } : {
        tickvals: PTN_VALS, ticktext: PTN_TICK_LBLS,
        title: { text: 'Post-Test N', font: { size: 12 }, standoff: 4 },
        showgrid: true, gridcolor: '#eee'
      },
      yaxis: {
        title: { text: isAlpha ? 'Optimal Pr(Best)' : (isDays ? 'Avg Days to Stop' : 'Marginal Events vs No Test'), font: { size: 12 } },
        tickformat: isAlpha ? '.2f' : (isDays ? '.1f' : ',.0f'),
        range: isAlpha ? [0.43, 1.02] : (isDays ? [0, 22] : undefined),
        showgrid: true, gridcolor: '#eee'
      },
      annotations: annotations,
      legend: { orientation: 'h', x: 0.5, xanchor: 'center', y: -0.22, font: { size: 11 },
                title: { text: '— click to show/hide —', font: { size: 11, color: '#555' }, side: 'top' },
                bordercolor: '#999', borderwidth: 1.5,
                bgcolor: 'white' },
      margin: { t: 20, b: 100, l: isAlpha ? 55 : (isDays ? 55 : 75), r: (isAlpha || isDays) ? 55 : 15 },
      height: 340, paper_bgcolor: 'white', plot_bgcolor: 'white',
      hoverlabel: { font: { size: 16, color: 'white' } }
    }, { responsive: true, displayModeBar: false });
  }

  var BULLETS = {
    alpha: [],
    marginal: [],
    days: []
  };

  function updateTakeaways(metric) {
    var ul  = document.getElementById('ptn-takeaways');
    var box = document.getElementById('ptn-takeaways-box');
    if (!ul) return;
    var bullets = BULLETS[metric] || [];
    ul.innerHTML = bullets.map(function (b) { return '<li>' + b + '</li>'; }).join('');
    if (box) box.style.display = bullets.length ? '' : 'none';
  }

  function update() {
    var metric = document.getElementById('ptn-metric').value;
    buildChart(
      document.getElementById('ptn-cr').value,
      document.getElementById('ptn-sigma').value,
      metric
    );
    updateTakeaways(metric);
  }

  ['ptn-cr', 'ptn-sigma', 'ptn-metric'].forEach(function (id) {
    document.getElementById(id).addEventListener('change', update);
  });

  if (document.readyState === 'loading') {
    document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', update);
  } else {
    setTimeout(update, 0);
  }
})();

// Sync Baseline Conversion and Expected Effects dropdowns between Results and Importance sections
(function () {
  var _syncing = false;
  function syncTo(srcId, dstId) {
    var src = document.getElementById(srcId);
    var dst = document.getElementById(dstId);
    if (!src || !dst) return;
    src.addEventListener('change', function () {
      if (_syncing || dst.value === src.value) return;
      _syncing = true;
      dst.value = src.value;
      dst.dispatchEvent(new Event('change'));
      _syncing = false;
    });
  }
  syncTo('wif-cr',    'ptn-cr');
  syncTo('ptn-cr',    'wif-cr');
  syncTo('wif-sigma', 'ptn-sigma');
  syncTo('ptn-sigma', 'wif-sigma');
})();
</script>
```

# Code Snippets

Six reusable pieces from the analysis — the full pipeline from raw simulation through the test statistics that drive every stopping decision. All fully vectorized — no for loops.

```{=html}
<div class="mt-3 mb-2">
  <div class="accordion-item" style="border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-radius: 6px; background: #f0f6fd; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;">
    <div class="accordion-header" style="margin:0;">
      <button class="accordion-button collapsed px-3 shadow-none"
              style="font-size:0.9rem; font-weight:600; color:#1e46a0; background:transparent; height:auto; width:100%; display:flex; align-items:center; padding-top:0.5rem; padding-bottom:0.5rem; flex-wrap:wrap;"
              type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse" data-bs-target="#code-control-collapse">
        <span style="flex:1;">Simulating the Control</span>
        <span class="accordion-chevron" style="flex-shrink:0; margin-left:0.75rem; font-size:1.8rem; line-height:1; transition:transform 0.2s;">&#8964;</span>
        <span style="width:100%; font-size:0.85rem; font-weight:400; font-style:italic; color:#555; margin-top:0.2rem;">Each simulation of control is an independent binomial process — same true rate, but different random outcome.</span>
      </button>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div id="code-control-collapse" class="collapse" style="border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-top: none; border-radius: 0 0 6px 6px; padding: 1rem; background: #fff; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;">
    <p style="font-size:0.9rem; margin-bottom:0.75rem;"><code>create_simulation_df</code> generates a DataFrame of daily binomial draws for one or more fixed-rate conditions. Each row is one day of one simulation.</p>
    <pre style="background:#f8f8f8; border-radius:4px; padding:1rem; font-size:0.8rem; overflow-x:auto;"><code>import numpy as np
import polars as pl


def create_simulation_df(
    number_test_days,
    number_simulations,
    condition_parameters,
    seed=None,
):
    if seed is not None:
        np.random.seed(seed)

    # trials_per_day is the same for every day in the experiment
    n = np.array(
        [c["trials"] // number_test_days for c in condition_parameters]
    )[:, None]
    p = np.array([c["mean"] for c in condition_parameters])[:, None]

    rep = number_test_days * number_simulations
    n_mat = np.repeat(n, rep, axis=1)
    p_mat = np.repeat(p, rep, axis=1)

    # One binomial draw per (condition, day, simulation)
    samples = np.random.binomial(n=n_mat, p=p_mat)

    return pl.DataFrame(
        {
            "simulation": np.arange(rep) // number_test_days + 1,
            "day": np.arange(rep) % number_test_days + 1,
            **{
                f"condition_{i}_value": samples[i]
                for i in range(len(condition_parameters))
            },
            **{
                f"condition_{i}_trials": n_mat[i]
                for i in range(len(condition_parameters))
            },
            **{
                f"condition_{i}_true_mean": np.full(
                    rep, condition_parameters[i]["mean"]
                )
                for i in range(len(condition_parameters))
            },
        }
    )</code></pre>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="mt-3 mb-2">
  <div class="accordion-item" style="border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-radius: 6px; background: #f0f6fd; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;">
    <div class="accordion-header" style="margin:0;">
      <button class="accordion-button collapsed px-3 shadow-none"
              style="font-size:0.9rem; font-weight:600; color:#1e46a0; background:transparent; height:auto; width:100%; display:flex; align-items:center; padding-top:0.5rem; padding-bottom:0.5rem; flex-wrap:wrap;"
              type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse" data-bs-target="#code-treatment-collapse">
        <span style="flex:1;">Simulating the Treatment</span>
        <span class="accordion-chevron" style="flex-shrink:0; margin-left:0.75rem; font-size:1.8rem; line-height:1; transition:transform 0.2s;">&#8964;</span>
        <span style="width:100%; font-size:0.85rem; font-weight:400; font-style:italic; color:#555; margin-top:0.2rem;">Each simulation of treatment is encoded with a different true effect — sometimes harmful, sometimes beneficial, often marginal.</span>
      </button>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div id="code-treatment-collapse" class="collapse" style="border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-top: none; border-radius: 0 0 6px 6px; padding: 1rem; background: #fff; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;">
    <p style="font-size:0.9rem; margin-bottom:0.75rem;"><code>add_random_effect_condition</code> appends a treatment arm where the true effect varies across simulations but is fixed within one. The effect is drawn from an ITC (Index-to-Control) distribution: ITC = 100 &#8594; no lift; ITC ~ N(100, 1) &#8594; effects centered at parity with modest spread.</p>
    <pre style="background:#f8f8f8; border-radius:4px; padding:1rem; font-size:0.8rem; overflow-x:auto;"><code>import numpy as np
import polars as pl


def add_random_effect_condition(df, random_effect_parameters, seed=None):
    if seed is not None:
        np.random.seed(seed)

    n_simulations = int(df["simulation"].max())
    n_days = int(df["day"].max())

    # One ITC draw per simulation — the unknown true effect for that experiment
    itc_samples = (
        np.random.normal(
            random_effect_parameters["itc_mean"],
            random_effect_parameters["itc_se"],
            n_simulations,
        )
        / 100
    )

    # treatment_mean = (itc / 100) x control_mean
    control_mean = float(df["condition_0_true_mean"][0])
    treatment_means = itc_samples * control_mean

    # Daily binomial draws; treatment_mean is constant across days within a simulation
    trials_per_day = random_effect_parameters["trials"] // n_days
    events = np.random.binomial(
        n=trials_per_day,
        p=treatment_means[:, None],
        size=(n_simulations, n_days),
    )

    # Reshape to tall format to match the existing DataFrame
    next_condition = (
        max(
            int(c.split("_")[1])
            for c in df.columns
            if c.startswith("condition_") and c.split("_")[1].isdigit()
        )
        + 1
    )
    return df.with_columns(
        [
            pl.Series(
                f"condition_{next_condition}_value",
                events.flatten(),
            ),
            pl.Series(
                f"condition_{next_condition}_trials",
                np.full(n_simulations * n_days, trials_per_day),
            ),
            pl.Series(
                f"condition_{next_condition}_true_mean",
                np.repeat(treatment_means, n_days),
            ),
        ]
    )</code></pre>
  </div>
</div>
<div class="mt-3 mb-2">
  <div class="accordion-item" style="border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-radius: 6px; background: #f0f6fd; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;">
    <div class="accordion-header" style="margin:0;">
      <button class="accordion-button collapsed px-3 shadow-none"
              style="font-size:0.9rem; font-weight:600; color:#1e46a0; background:transparent; height:auto; width:100%; display:flex; align-items:center; padding-top:0.5rem; padding-bottom:0.5rem; flex-wrap:wrap;"
              type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse" data-bs-target="#code-cumulative-collapse">
        <span style="flex:1;">Computing Cumulative Stats</span>
        <span class="accordion-chevron" style="flex-shrink:0; margin-left:0.75rem; font-size:1.8rem; line-height:1; transition:transform 0.2s;">&#8964;</span>
        <span style="width:100%; font-size:0.85rem; font-weight:400; font-style:italic; color:#555; margin-top:0.2rem;">Converts daily draws into running totals — the input to every stopping rule.</span>
      </button>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div id="code-cumulative-collapse" class="collapse" style="border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-top: none; border-radius: 0 0 6px 6px; padding: 1rem; background: #fff; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;">
    <p style="font-size:0.9rem; margin-bottom:0.75rem;"><code>add_cumulative_columns</code> converts daily event counts into running totals grouped by simulation. Each condition gets a cumulative event count, trial count, and conversion rate. Cross-condition totals and remaining trials are derived in a single vectorized pass — no loops.</p>
    <pre style="background:#f8f8f8; border-radius:4px; padding:1rem; font-size:0.8rem; overflow-x:auto;"><code>import polars as pl


def add_cumulative_columns(df):
    value_cols  = [col for col in df.columns if col.endswith("_value")]
    trials_cols = [col for col in df.columns if col.endswith("_trials")]

    # Running totals per condition, grouped by simulation
    cumsum_exprs = [
        pl.col(col).cum_sum().over("simulation").alias(f"{col}_cumsum")
        for col in value_cols + trials_cols
    ]
    df = df.with_columns(cumsum_exprs)

    # Cross-condition totals
    value_cumsums  = [f"{c}_cumsum" for c in value_cols]
    trials_cumsums = [f"{c}_cumsum" for c in trials_cols]
    df = df.with_columns([
        pl.sum_horizontal(value_cumsums).alias("total_value_cumsum"),
        pl.sum_horizontal(trials_cumsums).alias("total_trials_cumsum"),
    ])

    # Trials remaining in the experiment window
    df = df.with_columns([
        (pl.col("total_trials_cumsum").max().over("simulation")
         - pl.col("total_trials_cumsum")).alias("total_remaining_test_trials")
    ])

    # Cumulative conversion rate per condition
    mean_exprs = [
        (pl.col(f"{c}_cumsum") / pl.col(f"{c.replace('_value','_trials')}_cumsum"))
        .alias(f"{c.replace('_value','')}_mean_cumsum")
        for c in value_cols
        if f"{c.replace('_value','_trials')}" in trials_cols
    ]
    return df.with_columns(mean_exprs)</code></pre>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="mt-3 mb-2">
  <div class="accordion-item" style="border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-radius: 6px; background: #f0f6fd; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;">
    <div class="accordion-header" style="margin:0;">
      <button class="accordion-button collapsed px-3 shadow-none"
              style="font-size:0.9rem; font-weight:600; color:#1e46a0; background:transparent; height:auto; width:100%; display:flex; align-items:center; padding-top:0.5rem; padding-bottom:0.5rem; flex-wrap:wrap;"
              type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse" data-bs-target="#code-std-collapse">
        <span style="flex:1;">Cumulative Standard Deviation</span>
        <span class="accordion-chevron" style="flex-shrink:0; margin-left:0.75rem; font-size:1.8rem; line-height:1; transition:transform 0.2s;">&#8964;</span>
        <span style="width:100%; font-size:0.85rem; font-weight:400; font-style:italic; color:#555; margin-top:0.2rem;">Binomial std of the running conversion rate — input to z-scores and confidence intervals.</span>
      </button>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div id="code-std-collapse" class="collapse" style="border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-top: none; border-radius: 0 0 6px 6px; padding: 1rem; background: #fff; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;">
    <p style="font-size:0.9rem; margin-bottom:0.75rem;"><code>compute_cumulative_std_rates</code> applies the binomial standard deviation formula <code>sqrt(p * (1 - p))</code> to each condition's running conversion rate. Note: this is the <em>std of the proportion</em>, not the standard error — divide by <code>sqrt(n)</code> to get SE.</p>
    <pre style="background:#f8f8f8; border-radius:4px; padding:1rem; font-size:0.8rem; overflow-x:auto;"><code>import polars as pl


def compute_cumulative_std_rates(df):
    mean_cumsum_cols = [col for col in df.columns if col.endswith("_mean_cumsum")]

    std_exprs = [
        (pl.col(col) * (1 - pl.col(col)))
        .sqrt()
        .alias(col.replace("_mean_cumsum", "_std_cumsum"))
        for col in mean_cumsum_cols
    ]
    return df.with_columns(std_exprs)</code></pre>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="mt-3 mb-2">
  <div class="accordion-item" style="border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-radius: 6px; background: #f0f6fd; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;">
    <div class="accordion-header" style="margin:0;">
      <button class="accordion-button collapsed px-3 shadow-none"
              style="font-size:0.9rem; font-weight:600; color:#1e46a0; background:transparent; height:auto; width:100%; display:flex; align-items:center; padding-top:0.5rem; padding-bottom:0.5rem; flex-wrap:wrap;"
              type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse" data-bs-target="#code-delta-collapse">
        <span style="flex:1;">Computing Delta Statistics</span>
        <span class="accordion-chevron" style="flex-shrink:0; margin-left:0.75rem; font-size:1.8rem; line-height:1; transition:transform 0.2s;">&#8964;</span>
        <span style="width:100%; font-size:0.85rem; font-weight:400; font-style:italic; color:#555; margin-top:0.2rem;">Treatment effect size and combined standard error — inputs to z-scores and p-values.</span>
      </button>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div id="code-delta-collapse" class="collapse" style="border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-top: none; border-radius: 0 0 6px 6px; padding: 1rem; background: #fff; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;">
    <p style="font-size:0.9rem; margin-bottom:0.75rem;"><code>compute_delta_stats</code> computes two things for each treatment condition vs control: the standard error (<code>SE = std / sqrt(n)</code>) and the delta statistics (<code>delta_mean = mean_i - mean_0</code>, <code>delta_se = sqrt(SE_i² + SE_0²)</code>). These are the direct inputs to z-scores and p-values — this function does not compute those itself.</p>
    <pre style="background:#f8f8f8; border-radius:4px; padding:1rem; font-size:0.8rem; overflow-x:auto;"><code>import polars as pl


def compute_delta_stats(df):
    mean_cols = [c for c in df.columns if c.endswith("_mean_cumsum")]

    # SE = std / sqrt(trials) for every condition
    se_exprs = [
        (pl.col(f"condition_{i}_std_cumsum") / pl.col(f"condition_{i}_trials_cumsum").sqrt())
        .alias(f"condition_{i}_se_cumsum")
        for i in [int(c.split("_")[1]) for c in mean_cols if c.startswith("condition_")]
        if f"condition_{i}_std_cumsum" in df.columns
    ]
    df = df.with_columns(se_exprs)

    # Delta mean and delta SE for each treatment vs condition_0 (control)
    treatment_nums = [
        int(c.split("_")[1]) for c in mean_cols
        if c.startswith("condition_") and int(c.split("_")[1]) > 0
    ]
    delta_exprs = []
    for i in treatment_nums:
        delta_exprs += [
            (pl.col(f"condition_{i}_mean_cumsum") - pl.col("condition_0_mean_cumsum"))
            .alias(f"condition_{i}_delta_mean"),
            (pl.col(f"condition_{i}_se_cumsum").pow(2) + pl.col("condition_0_se_cumsum").pow(2))
            .sqrt()
            .alias(f"condition_{i}_delta_se"),
        ]
    return df.with_columns(delta_exprs)</code></pre>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="mt-3 mb-2">
  <div class="accordion-item" style="border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-radius: 6px; background: #f0f6fd; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;">
    <div class="accordion-header" style="margin:0;">
      <button class="accordion-button collapsed px-3 shadow-none"
              style="font-size:0.9rem; font-weight:600; color:#1e46a0; background:transparent; height:auto; width:100%; display:flex; align-items:center; padding-top:0.5rem; padding-bottom:0.5rem; flex-wrap:wrap;"
              type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse" data-bs-target="#code-teststats-collapse">
        <span style="flex:1;">Computing Test Statistics</span>
        <span class="accordion-chevron" style="flex-shrink:0; margin-left:0.75rem; font-size:1.8rem; line-height:1; transition:transform 0.2s;">&#8964;</span>
        <span style="width:100%; font-size:0.85rem; font-weight:400; font-style:italic; color:#555; margin-top:0.2rem;">Converts delta stats into Pr(Best) and expected gain/loss — the signal the stopping rule acts on.</span>
      </button>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div id="code-teststats-collapse" class="collapse" style="border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-top: none; border-radius: 0 0 6px 6px; padding: 1rem; background: #fff; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;">
    <p style="font-size:0.9rem; margin-bottom:0.75rem;"><code>compute_test_stats</code> converts delta statistics into probabilities and expected values using the normal CDF. For each treatment: <code>Pr(treatment &gt; control) = Φ(delta_mean / delta_se)</code>. Expected gain and loss use truncated normal formulas. These are the columns the stopping rule reads — it stops when <code>Pr(Best)</code> crosses the threshold α.</p>
    <pre style="background:#f8f8f8; border-radius:4px; padding:1rem; font-size:0.8rem; overflow-x:auto;"><code>import numpy as np
import polars as pl
from scipy.stats import norm


def compute_test_stats(df):
    delta_mean_cols = [c for c in df.columns if c.endswith("_delta_mean")]
    condition_nums  = [int(c.split("_")[1]) for c in delta_mean_cols]

    pr_cols = []
    for i in condition_nums:
        z = df[f"condition_{i}_delta_mean"].to_numpy() / df[f"condition_{i}_delta_se"].to_numpy()
        pr = norm.cdf(z)
        df = df.with_columns([
            pl.lit(pr).alias(f"condition_{i}_pr_positive_diff"),
            pl.lit(1 - pr).alias(f"condition_{i}_pr_lowest_diff"),
        ])
        pr_cols.append(f"condition_{i}_pr_positive_diff")

        # Expected gain and loss (truncated normal)
        pdf_z = norm.pdf(z)
        df = df.with_columns([
            pl.lit(np.abs(pr   * (df[f"condition_{i}_delta_mean"].to_numpy() + df[f"condition_{i}_delta_se"].to_numpy() * pdf_z / np.maximum(pr,     1e-10)))).alias(f"condition_{i}_expected_gain"),
            pl.lit(np.abs((1-pr) * (-df[f"condition_{i}_delta_mean"].to_numpy() + df[f"condition_{i}_delta_se"].to_numpy() * pdf_z / np.maximum(1-pr, 1e-10)))).alias(f"condition_{i}_expected_loss"),
        ])

    # Control: Pr(best) = 1 - max(Pr(treatment > control))
    if pr_cols:
        df = df.with_columns([
            (1 - pl.max_horizontal(pr_cols)).alias("condition_0_pr_highest_diff"),
            pl.min_horizontal(pr_cols).alias("condition_0_pr_lowest_diff"),
        ])
    return df</code></pre>
  </div>
</div>

<p style="margin-top:1rem; font-size:0.95rem; color:#555;">Want to see more? <a href="mailto:rzaliznyak@gmail.com">Let me know</a>.</p>
```
